daily_brief: The NATO summit opening this week comes amid urgent appeals from Ukraine, where a massive Russian barrage on Kyiv killed at least 14 people on Sunday night, with Ukrainian officials warning of a 'serious shortage' of interceptor missiles that left 23 ballistic missiles undefended [BBC]. President Zelensky has intensified calls for allied air defense systems while Ukraine continues striking Russian energy infrastructure, hitting a major oil terminal in St. Petersburg [BBC]. In France, attention turns to Tuesday's appeal verdict for Marine Le Pen, which will determine whether the far-right leader can contest the 2027 presidential election [BBC, France 24]. Meanwhile, wildfires burning out of control near the French-Spanish border have forced the evacuation of 10,000 people and disrupted the Tour de France, with officials warning early summer heatwaves have accelerated fire season across southern Europe [France 24, RFI]. China's test-firing of an intercontinental ballistic missile into the South Pacific drew sharp criticism from Australia and New Zealand, who called the launch inconsistent with regional stability [Globe and Mail, FT]. At the World Cup, FIFA's controversial decision to suspend US striker Folarin Balogun's red card ban—reportedly following White House intervention—has sparked Belgian protests and a formal appeal hearing [France 24, Al Jazeera].

top_stories.headline: Russia Kills 14 in Kyiv Barrage as Ukraine Warns of Critical Air Defense Shortage Before NATO Summit
bullets: Russian forces launched 68 missiles and 351 strike drones at Kyiv overnight Sunday; none of the 23 ballistic missiles were intercepted due to what Ukrainian Air Force called a 'serious shortage' of interceptor missiles
At least 14 people killed in the Kyiv region, with three apartment blocks partially collapsed and rescue operations ongoing to reach those trapped
President Zelensky appealed for allies to make 'strong decisions' at this week's NATO summit in Turkey, warning attacks will continue while Patriot missiles 'remain in our allies' stockpiles'
Ukraine simultaneously struck a major oil terminal in St. Petersburg, approximately 850km from the Ukrainian border, continuing its campaign against Russian energy infrastructure

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Emphasized humanitarian toll and rescue efforts alongside military context

top_stories.headline: Marine Le Pen's Political Future Hangs on Tuesday Appeal Verdict in Embezzlement Case
bullets: Paris appeal court will rule Tuesday on whether to uphold Le Pen's March 2025 conviction for embezzling €1.4 million in European Parliament funds to pay National Rally party employees
Original conviction included a five-year ban from holding public office, which would bar her from the April 2027 presidential election where she currently leads polls
If upheld, Jordan Bardella would likely stand as National Rally's presidential candidate in her place
Le Pen denied organizing the fake jobs scheme during January-February appeal hearings

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Connected to broader National Rally legal troubles, citing Mediapart report on additional irregularities under Bardella

top_stories.headline: Wildfires Force 10,000 to Evacuate in Southwest France as Early Fire Season Hits Europe
bullets: A wildfire near the French-Spanish border has burned approximately 4,600 hectares and forced evacuation of 10,000 people from two dozen towns and villages in the Pyrénées foothills
Over 700 firefighters with water-bombing aircraft are battling the blaze; officials warned strong Monday winds would further fan flames
Tour de France stage three was closed to spectators to allow emergency service access, though the race will proceed with a reduced vehicle convoy
Early heatwaves in May and June have left vegetation across southern Europe particularly dry, with officials warning fire season started a month earlier than usual

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of evacuations and Tour de France impact

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Broader European context, noting over 19,000 hectares burned across France, Portugal, Spain, and Greece

top_stories.headline: China Fires Submarine-Launched ICBM into Pacific, Drawing Regional Condemnation
bullets: People's Liberation Army Navy announced a nuclear-powered submarine fired a 'strategic missile carrying a dummy warhead' into the Pacific just after midday Beijing time Monday
New Zealand Foreign Minister Winston Peters called the test 'deeply concerning' and 'not consistent with regional stability,' noting China informed regional governments only hours before launch
The missile was fired into the South Pacific Nuclear Free Zone established by the Treaty of Rarotonga, to which China is a partial signatory
This marks China's first ICBM test in the Pacific since September 2024, when a similar launch raised regional alarms about Beijing's military expansion

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Detailed diplomatic reaction from Australia and New Zealand

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Headline focused on nuclear-free zone violation (paywall limited details)

top_stories.headline: FIFA Suspends Balogun Red Card Ban After Reported Trump Intervention; Belgium Appeals
bullets: FIFA announced Sunday it was suspending the one-match ban for US striker Folarin Balogun, who received a direct red card and was set to miss Monday's round of 16 match against Belgium
The decision reportedly followed intervention from US President Donald Trump, triggering widespread debate and a formal protest from the Royal Belgian Football Association
Belgium was granted the right to appeal, with both federations asked to submit arguments by 5am PT Monday—12 hours before the scheduled kickoff at Seattle Stadium
Balogun, who has scored three World Cup goals, is currently eligible to play and expected to start

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized Trump's lobbying role in press review context

additional_stories.headline: Millions attend funeral procession for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, with crowds calling for revenge and some demanding Trump's assassination
Macron to become first Western European leader to visit post-Assad Syria, with Damascus announcing trip to discuss bilateral relations and economic cooperation
At least 20 people killed and over 100 wounded in violent clashes at Sri Lanka's Negombo prison
Trump offers to help Putin resolve Ukraine war during 90-minute Fourth of July phone call, Kremlin aide confirms
Venezuela earthquake death toll tops 3,300 as dozens of unidentified bodies are buried; 12-year-old girl rescued after 32 hours trapped under rubble
Floods in Côte d'Ivoire have killed 59 people since May as deadly rains continue across coastal West Africa
Impeachment trial begins for Philippines Vice President Sara Duterte over alleged misuse of funds and threats against President Marcos
EasyJet reaches outline agreement on £5.5 billion takeover by private equity firm Castlelake
RSF drone strikes kill at least 45 in Sudan's besieged El Obeid as UN warns of atrocity risks
Novak Djokovic breaks Roger Federer's record for most men's singles wins at Wimbledon with 106th victory
